name: Cathe's Burgundy-Mushroom Gravy
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Mushrooms (fresh), cleaned, trimmed and sliced thinly
1 cup Burgundy wine (or other red wine)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the butter in a small pan (about 1-quart size).
2. Add the mushrooms to the pan and cook over medium heat until all liquid has evaporated and only the oil from the butter remains in the pan.
3. Stir frequently so they brown but do not burn.
4. Add the chopped onion and red wine. Continue cooking until all liquid has cooked out, leaving only the oil from the butter with the mushrooms and onions.
5. Add the flour to the mushroom mixture. Stir well so that the oil is absorbed by the flour.
6. Add the entire can of chicken broth at once and stir well. The broth and flour/butter mixture should combine into a thick liquid; it may not thicken until boiling begins. Stir constantly to prevent sticking.
7. Add water to achieve desired consistency (e.g., add 1 cup for a thicker gravy). Lower the heat.
8. Add garlic flakes, stirring on low heat until they absorb enough liquid to no longer be crunchy.
Cathe's notes:
Created in California, 1976.
Vicki's notes:
When Cathe first gave me this recipe (mid '80s), it recommended sauteing onions in olive oil, removing them, cooking half a pound of mushrooms until brown and "squeaky," adding half the wine, reducing, then adding the rest, returning onions with 1 tablespoon butter and stirring frequently until very brown. Add ¼ cup flour, stir until brown, add remaining broth, cook to gentle bubble, and thicken. If too thick, add up to ½ cup water and simmer until flour taste is gone.
